title: Inline <style> tags in PHP output
summary: Inline style tags in PHP-rendered output are a common WordPress.org review blocker and should be replaced with enqueued assets or properly attached inline styles.

detection.notes: Current repo coverage is heuristic grep-based matching. A future engine should support suppression notes and safer context detection.

guidance.why_it_matters: Inline CSS is commonly flagged because WordPress plugins are expected to enqueue assets in the standard WordPress way.
guidance.fix: Move CSS to an enqueued stylesheet and use wp_add_inline_style() only when truly necessary.
guidance.false_positive_risk: Medium. Literal examples, docs, or escaped markup inside strings may be flagged.
